message:
fix: pin module fetch URL to release tag, not main (#43)

Closes #43. UBS pins per-language module checksums in MODULE_CHECKSUMS,
but REPO_RAW pointed at the main branch — so the moment main advanced
past the release the user had installed, every module download
mismatched its pinned digest, supply-chain verification rejected the
file, and the runner exited without scanning. The retry loop was
futile because each redownload pulled the same drifted main content.

Reproduce on any 5.0.7 / 5.1.1 install once main has touched a module:

    ✗ failed to verify module js: checksum mismatch for js
      (expected <pinned-in-binary>, got <current-main>)

Fix:

- Split REPO_RAW into two URLs. REPO_RAW (release-pinned, for module +
  helper fetches) now resolves to .../v${UBS_VERSION}/..., so an
  installed copy of v5.1.2 only ever pulls from the v5.1.2 tag and
  the pinned digests stay valid forever.
- REPO_RAW_LATEST (main) keeps the self-update probe pointed at main
  so a stale install can still discover a newer release; without that,
  comparing against our own pinned tag would loop forever.
- Bump UBS_VERSION (and VERSION file) to 5.1.2 so existing 5.1.1
  installs see a newer version on their next self-update probe and
  pull this fix automatically.

Verification:
- bash -n ubs (syntax)
- scripts/verify_checksums.sh (all green)
- ./ubs --version prints 5.1.2

Released as v5.1.2 to unblock all currently-installed users — they'll
self-update from main on their next run and land on the tag-pinned
launcher.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -8,6 +8,14 @@ Repository: <https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/ultimate_bug_scanner>
 
 ---
 
+## [v5.1.2] - 2026-04-25 **[Release]**
+
+### Bug fix
+
+- **Module fetch URL pinned to release tag instead of `main`** — every installed copy of UBS broke as soon as `main` advanced past the release the user had installed (issue #43). The launcher pins per-language module checksums, but `REPO_RAW` pointed at `main/modules/…`, so the moment any module changed on `main` the checksum verification rejected the download and the scanner refused to start. Fixed by pinning `REPO_RAW` to `v${UBS_VERSION}/…` for module/helper fetches while keeping the self-update probe on `main` (so stale installs still discover newer releases). Resolves the "expected `<pin>`, got `<current main>`" failure reported on macOS / Homebrew installs.
+
+---
+
 ## [v5.0.7] - 2026-03-25 **[Release]**
 
 > 80 commits since v5.0.6 — biggest release since v5.0.0.

@@ -28,8 +28,16 @@ set -Eeuo pipefail
 shopt -s lastpipe || true
 trap '' SIGPIPE 2>/dev/null || true
 
-UBS_VERSION="5.1.1"
-REPO_RAW="https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Dicklesworthstone/ultimate_bug_scanner/main"
+UBS_VERSION="5.1.2"
+# Module / helper fetches must be checksum-stable for an installed
+# release, so they pin to the tagged tree matching this script's
+# UBS_VERSION. main is reserved for the self-update probe below
+# (which is what advances a stale install onto a newer tag).
+# Issue #43: pinning to "main" caused every installed copy to break
+# the moment main moved past the release the user installed.
+REPO_RAW_BASE="https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Dicklesworthstone/ultimate_bug_scanner"
+REPO_RAW="${REPO_RAW_BASE}/v${UBS_VERSION}"
+REPO_RAW_LATEST="${REPO_RAW_BASE}/main"
 MODULE_PATH_TEMPLATE="$REPO_RAW/modules/ubs-%s.sh"
 
 # Known-good module digests (sha256) for supply-chain verification.
@@ -2123,7 +2131,10 @@ check_and_update_self(){
   if [[ ! -w "$0" ]]; then return 0; fi
 
   local self_path="$0"
-  local remote_url="$REPO_RAW/ubs"
+  # Self-update intentionally probes main (REPO_RAW_LATEST) rather than
+  # the release-pinned REPO_RAW — otherwise we'd compare against our
+  # own pinned tag and never see a newer release.
+  local remote_url="$REPO_RAW_LATEST/ubs"
   local last_check_file="${XDG_CACHE_HOME:-$HOME/.cache}/ubs/last_update_check"
   ensure_dir "$(dirname "$last_check_file")"
